summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on Kyiv and surrounding regions, deploying 90 missiles and 600 drones, of which Ukrainian air defenses downed 604 targets. Significant missile strikes also hit Bila Tserkva, Chernihiv, and other locations, while Ukrainian forces reported limited tactical bomber attacks in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. Casualties include at least 21 injured and one dead in Kyiv, with extensive damage to civilian infrastructure and cultural sites.
key_points: Russia's combined strike on Kyiv used 90 missiles (Iskander, Kinzhal, Zircon, etc.) and 600 Shahed-type drones; Ukrainian defenses shot down 604 targets (55 missiles, 549 drones) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Over 40 locations in Kyiv were hit, causing one fatality in Shevchenkivskyi district and 21 injuries, plus metro and museum damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Bila Tserkva was struck by the "Oreshnik" missile system, damaging a garage cooperative and enterprise buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
Russian tactical bombers launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Multiple cruise missile launches were reported from Chernihiv towards Lubny district, Poltava, and from Cherkasy towards northern areas (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67202, @mon1tor_ua/67174, @kpszsu/62621).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 11 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles and 44 cruise missiles (X-101/Iskander-K/Caliber) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Significant cultural sites damaged in Kyiv include the National Art Museum, Chernobyl Museum, and historic district structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81421).
Ukrainian intelligence reported reactive Shahed drones over Obukhiv, Chernihiv, and near the Desna River (Source: @kpszsu/62649, @mon1tor_ua/67206).

sections.executive_summary: Russia executed a massive combined airstrike on Kyiv, deploying 90 missiles and 600 drones, with Ukrainian defenses downing 604 targets. The attack caused civilian casualties, extensive infrastructure damage, and hit cultural sites, while additional missile strikes hit Bila Tserkva and other regions. Ukrainian forces responded with tactical bomber strikes in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk and continued air defense interceptions.

points: Ukrainian air defense shot down 55 missiles, including 11 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles and 44 cruise missiles (X-101/Iskander-K/Caliber), preventing further damage to Kyiv and surrounding areas (Source: @kpszsu/62647).

Casualties in Kyiv total one civilian death in Shevchenkivskyi district and 21 injured, with three in critical condition; metro stations Lukiivska and Khreshchatyk entrances were temporarily closed (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).

Cultural heritage sites suffered damage: National Art Museum, Chernobyl Museum, and historic buildings in the Ancient Kyiv reserve, though museum collections remain intact (Source: @UkraineNow/81421).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, targeting enemy positions (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of a Shahed drone over Obukhiv, mitigating potential strikes on civilian areas (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67206).
Ukrainian radar units observed cruise missile trajectories from Chernihiv toward Lubny district in Poltava, prompting heightened air defense alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67202).
Ukrainian command noted increased drone activity near the Desna River in Chernihiv, indicating potential reconnaissance or strike preparation (Source: @kpszsu/62649).
Ukrainian authorities continue to monitor and report missile debris falls across 23 locations, aiding damage assessment and civilian safety measures (Source: @kpszsu/62647).

Russia launched a coordinated strike on Kyiv using 1 medium-range ballistic missile from Kapustin Yar, 2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles from Lipetsk, 3 Zircon anti-ship missiles from Crimea, 30 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 54 cruise missiles (X-101/Iskander-K/Caliber) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).

A total of 600 Shahed-type attack drones, including variants Gerbera, Italmas, and loitering munitions, were launched from multiple Russian regions targeting Kyiv and other Ukrainian centers (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
The "Oreshnik" missile system struck Bila Tserkva, damaging a garage cooperative and enterprise structures, though exact impact coordinates were not disclosed (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
Russian forces employed cruise missiles from Chernihiv toward Lubny district in Poltava, indicating a broader southern thrust (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67202).

Additional cruise missile launches were reported from central Cherkasy toward northern Cherkasy, and from northern Chernihiv toward Slavutych, expanding the missile threat envelope (Source: @kpszsu/62621, @kpszsu/62633).
Russian tactical bombers conducted guided aerial bomb runs over Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, aiming to degrade Ukrainian defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Russian air assets also deployed reactive Shahed drones over Obukhiv and near the Desna River, suggesting ongoing low‑altitude strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62649, @mon1tor_ua/67206).
Russian propaganda outlets claimed the strike was retaliation for alleged Ukrainian attacks on Russian civilian targets, framing the operation as a defensive response (Source: @tass_agency/377297).
Cruise missiles launched from Chernihiv headed south toward Lubny district in Poltava, indicating a coordinated eastern thrust (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67202).
Russian tactical bombers dropped guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia, targeting infrastructure and troop concentrations (Source: @kpszsu/62631).
Additional cruise missile trajectories were observed from central Cherkasy moving northward, potentially threatening eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62621).
Shahed drones were reported over Obukhiv, a strategic point east of Kyiv, suggesting reconnaissance for upcoming operations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67206).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted multiple missile types, reducing the impact on eastern command posts (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian missile launches from the Crimean peninsula included Zircon anti-ship missiles, posing a threat to Black Sea naval assets supporting eastern operations (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian forces reported debris from downed missiles across 23 locations, indicating widespread missile dispersion in the east (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
No significant ground engagements were reported in the eastern sector during this timeframe.
Cruise missiles launched from northern Chernihiv were directed toward Slavutych, a key northern suburb of Kyiv, increasing pressure on northern defenses (Source: @kpszsu/62633).
Shahed drone groups were observed over northern Chernihiv moving toward Slavutych, indicating coordinated aerial attacks (Source: @kpszsu/62633).
Ukrainian radar detected a group of drones over Odesa region moving north, suggesting potential redeployment to the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/62658).
Russian missile launches from Vologda and Kursk regions targeted central Ukraine, but trajectories passed over northern oblasts, prompting heightened alerts (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses successfully intercepted 11 Iskander-M ballistic missiles, many of which originated from northern launch sites (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
No confirmed ground clashes were reported in the northern sector during the attack window.
Ukrainian forces continue to monitor drone activity near the Desna River, a strategic northern waterway (Source: @kpszsu/62649).
Civilian alerts were issued for multiple northern localities due to falling missile debris (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian missile launches from Kapustin Yar (Astrakhan region) and Lipetsk targeted Kyiv but also threatened southern approaches via long-range trajectories (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Shahed drones were reported over Obukhiv, a southern suburb of Kyiv, indicating southern penetration (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67206).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 44 cruise missiles, many of which were on southern flight paths toward central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
No direct missile impacts were reported in the southern oblasts of Odesa or Mykolaiv during this specific strike.
Ukrainian tactical bombers conducted strikes in Donetsk, a southern conflict zone, targeting Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62624).
Russian forces claimed to have hit a garage cooperative in Bila Tserkva, located south of Kyiv, causing infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
Ukrainian authorities reported debris from downed missiles in southern districts of Kyiv, prompting civilian safety measures (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Air defense units remained on high alert across the southern front due to the volume of incoming threats.
Ukrainian air defense shot down 55 missiles and 549 drones, totaling 604 targets, demonstrating high interception efficiency (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian air assets deployed 600 Shahed-type attack drones, overwhelming Ukrainian radar and requiring extensive engagement (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of 11 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 44 cruise missiles, reducing potential strategic damage (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian tactical bombers conducted guided aerial bomb runs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, indicating a shift to precision strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Multiple Shahed drones were observed over Obukhiv and Chernihiv, suggesting low‑altitude penetration tactics (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67206, @kpszsu/62649).
Ukrainian radar networks tracked cruise missile launches from multiple Russian regions, enabling timely defensive responses (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Airspace alerts were lifted in Kyiv at 07:46 on 24 May, indicating temporary cessation of the massive aerial barrage (Source: @eRadarrua/93468).
No significant Ukrainian aircraft losses were reported during this period.

key_developments: Russia executed a massive combined airstrike on Kyiv, launching 90 missiles (including Iskander, Kinzhal, Zircon) and 600 Shahed-type drones; Ukrainian defenses downed 604 targets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Over 40 locations in Kyiv were hit, causing one civilian death, 21 injuries, and damage to metro stations and cultural institutions (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
The "Oreshnik" missile system struck Bila Tserkva, damaging a garage cooperative and enterprise buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
Ukrainian tactical bombers dropped guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, targeting Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 11 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 44 cruise missiles, preventing further strategic damage (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Cruise missiles launched from Chernihiv headed toward Lubny district in Poltava, expanding the missile threat corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67202).
Shahed drones were observed over Obukhiv and near the Desna River, indicating ongoing low‑altitude strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67206, @kpszsu/62649).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces have demonstrated the capability to conduct large‑scale, coordinated aerial assaults using a mix of ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles combined with thousands of drones, overwhelming Ukrainian air defenses. The targeting of Kyiv's critical infrastructure and cultural sites indicates a strategic intent to erode morale and disrupt command‑and‑control. Continued missile launches from northern and central Russian regions, coupled with tactical bomber activity in the east and south, suggest a multi‑axis pressure strategy that will likely persist, requiring sustained Ukrainian air defense readiness.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have mobilized emergency services across Kyiv, reinforcing air defense units and conducting rapid damage assessments of cultural and civilian infrastructure. Regional commanders in Chernihiv, Poltava, and Cherkasy have heightened radar monitoring and prepared ground forces for potential follow‑on attacks. International partners are monitoring the escalation, with increased intelligence sharing to support Ukraine's air defense logistics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring confirms high‑volume missile and drone launches from multiple Russian launch sites, corroborating the scale of the attack. Debris fields across 23 locations provide valuable forensic data for missile type identification. Ukrainian interception success rates remain high, but the sheer volume of threats underscores the need for additional air defense assets and ammunition resupply. Continued observation of low‑altitude drone activity near strategic waterways suggests reconnaissance for future targeting cycles.
